chapter_context.summary: Prophesies the glorious future exaltation of the mountain of the Lord's house, where nations shall stream to learn His ways, beat their swords into plowshares, and sit under their own vines, predicting that though Zion must first go to Babylon, she will ultimately triumph.

outline.text: The Future Glory of the Temple Mount: Stream of Nations and the Universal Reign of Peace
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Future Glory of the Temple Mount: Stream of Nations and the Universal Reign of Peace (4:1-5), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Micah.

outline.text: The Gathering of the Lame and Afflicted: Exalting Mount Zion and Restoring Kingdom Dominion
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Gathering of the Lame and Afflicted: Exalting Mount Zion and Restoring Kingdom Dominion (4:6-8), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Micah.

outline.text: The Transition: The Pain of Exile in Babylon followed by the Gathering and Threshing of Surrounding Pagan Nations
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Transition: The Pain of Exile in Babylon followed by the Gathering and Threshing of Surrounding Pagan Nations (4:9-13), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Micah.

key_themes.title: True Worship as Ethical Integrity
key_themes.description: Micah clarifies that religious rituals are meaningless without ethical behavior in everyday commercial, legal, and social interactions.

key_themes.title: Messianic Hope and Bethlehem
key_themes.description: The book introduces the eternal ruler coming from Bethlehem, pointing to the ultimate messianic restoration and security of God's sheep.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the warnings and requirements in Chapter 4, what does it practically mean for you to walk humbly with God in your current season of life?
How does the final vision of a compassionate God casting our sins into the deep sea inspire your daily worship and forgiveness of others?

cultural_historical.title: The Southern Kingdom under Jotham, Ahaz, and Hezekiah
cultural_historical.text: Micah's ministry spanned the reigns of three kings of Judah, representing a period of political tension with Assyria, extreme corruption of the elite classes in Jerusalem, and eventually the reforming reign of Hezekiah.
